skill.name: x-collect
skill.description: Collects research materials for X content creation through a structured four-round web search workflow.
skill.summary: Research topics, sources, trends, and angles for X posts using progressive web search rounds.
skill.author: kangarooking

security_audit.summary: The static command execution and weak cryptography findings are false positives from Markdown text and fenced examples. The skill does intentionally use web search and describes saving results to a temporary file, so it carries moderate privacy and filesystem handling risk. No prompt injection, malicious intent, credential access, or executable code was found in SKILL.md.

medium_findings.title: User Topics Are Sent to Web Search
medium_findings.description: The skill requires WebSearch and an internet connection, then turns user topics into multiple search queries. This is expected behavior for the skill, but users should avoid sensitive, private, or embargoed topics.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly lists WebSearch and internet access as prerequisites and provides multiple search rounds. This confirms network disclosure of user-provided research topics.

medium_findings.title: Temporary File Handling Is Underspecified
medium_findings.description: The workflow says collected results should be saved to a temporary file for another skill to use. The file path, retention policy, and access controls are not defined, which may expose research notes or source lists on shared systems.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The temporary file handoff is directly documented, but no implementation is included. The risk depends on how the host tool writes and cleans up the file.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick locations are Markdown fenced blocks and example output. They contain search query templates, report formatting, and user-facing examples, not executable shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are visible Markdown examples with no interpreter, script file, or command execution API. The repeated hits come from fenced-code delimiters and pseudo commands.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The cited lines describe the skill and its required topic input. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, password hashing, encryption, signing, or other cryptographic operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain descriptive prose and the input heading only. There is no cryptographic code or algorithm reference in those locations.

content.value_statement: X content planning often needs current sources, comparisons, and topic angles before drafting starts. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through structured web research and produces a source-based material report.
actual_capabilities: Runs a four-round research workflow focused on official, technical, comparison, and gap-filling sources.
Creates search queries from a user-provided topic and optional language preference.
Prioritizes official documentation, GitHub projects, announcements, tutorials, reviews, and recent updates.
Summarizes collected materials into a structured report with source URLs and key points.
Identifies topic heat, trends, discussion focus, controversy points, and possible X post angles.
Suggests a follow-up filtering step with x-filter after material collection.
limitations: It depends on available web search tools and internet access.
Search results may reflect search engine ranking, source availability, and current indexing.
It does not verify private data, paid sources, or information behind login walls.
The described temporary file handoff needs host-side handling and cleanup.

use_cases.title: Research a Technical Launch
use_cases.description: Collect official announcements, docs, tutorials, and community reviews before writing posts about a new developer tool.

use_cases.title: Prepare Timely Trend Coverage
use_cases.description: Gather recent sources, discussion points, and debate angles for a fast-moving X topic.

use_cases.title: Build a Source-Based Brief
use_cases.description: Create a concise material report with URLs, summaries, and recommended post types for editorial planning.

prompt_templates.title: Collect Basic Topic Materials
prompt_templates.prompt: Use x-collect to research this topic for X content: [topic]. Use Chinese output and include source URLs.

prompt_templates.title: Research an English Launch Topic
prompt_templates.prompt: Use x-collect to gather materials about [product or feature launch]. Output in English and prioritize official sources first.

prompt_templates.title: Compare a Topic With Alternatives
prompt_templates.prompt: Use x-collect for [topic] and compare it with [competitor or alternative]. Highlight pros, cons, controversy, and post angles.

prompt_templates.title: Build an Advanced Content Brief
prompt_templates.scenario: Full research package for a planned X thread
prompt_templates.prompt: Use x-collect to research [topic] for an expert X thread. Include official sources, technical explainers, reviews, recent updates, gaps, heat analysis, controversies, and recommended post types.

output_examples.input: Use x-collect to research Claude MCP协议.
output_examples.output: A material report with official MCP sources, GitHub references, tutorials, security notes, recent updates, key points, and suggested X post angles.

output_examples.input: Collect X materials for a new AI coding agent launch.
output_examples.output: A research brief covering announcement sources, technical details, comparison points, market discussion, trend level, controversies, and next-step filtering guidance.

output_examples.input: Research a controversial AI policy topic for social posts.
output_examples.output: A balanced source collection with multiple perspectives, discussion focus, controversy points, current heat, and recommended post formats.

best_practices: Use specific topics, product names, version numbers, and language preferences.
Review source URLs before drafting posts or making factual claims.
Ask for recent updates when covering fast-moving technical or policy topics.
anti_patterns: Do not use sensitive private topics that should not become search queries.
Do not treat search summaries as verified facts without checking primary sources.
Do not skip comparison and controversy searches for topics with public debate.

faq.question: What does this skill produce?
faq.answer: It produces a structured material collection report for X content planning, including source URLs, summaries, trends, and post angles.

faq.question: Does it write final X posts?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on research collection and suggests using a filtering step before writing.

faq.question: Can it research current events?
faq.answer: Yes, if web search is available. Results depend on current indexing and source quality.

faq.question: What languages does it support?
faq.answer: The skill defaults to Chinese output and allows the user to request another output language.

faq.question: Is it safe to use with confidential topics?
faq.answer: Avoid confidential topics because the workflow sends user topics to web search services.

faq.question: Why is the audit risk level medium?
faq.answer: The skill is publishable, but it uses external web search and describes temporary file storage for collected materials.
